title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

methodology.description: Converts binary or scalar feedback from the environment into verbal feedback in the form of a textual summary, which is then added as additional context for the LLM agent in the next episode.
feedback_generation: Pre-defined heuristics for common failure cases
Self-evaluation using LLMs (decision-making) or self-written unit tests (programming)
methodology.key_concept: Self-reflective feedback acts as a 'semantic' gradient signal by providing the agent with a concrete direction to improve upon.

advantages: Lightweight and doesn't require finetuning the LLM
Allows for more nuanced forms of feedback (e.g. targeted changes in actions)
Provides explicit and interpretable episodic memory over prior experiences
Provides more explicit hints for actions in future episodes
disadvantages: Relies on the power of the LLM's self-evaluation capabilities (or heuristics)

task_types: Decision-making tasks (sequential action choices over long trajectories)
Programming tasks (teaching the agent to effectively use external tools)

results.decision_making: Improved on AlfWorld tasks by 22% over strong baseline approaches in 12 iterative learning steps
results.programming: Improved on Python programming tasks on HumanEval
